opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Interesting approach, but I've been building agents that need to run persistently and had to think hard about memory pressure and CPU spikes. The latency between user intent and agent response is what I find most challenging - even with local models, the system calls and context switching adds noticeable lag. I'm also curious how you handle the permission model - users need to trust your agent with their keystrokes and screen activity.
actions.reasoning: This is directly about an AI agent with OS-level integration, which touches on system architecture and agent execution environments.

opportunity.title: SysNav – An Intelligent Cockpit for DevOps (Local-First)
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft:
I can see the local-first approach being interesting for privacy and offline reliability, but I'd be curious about what "intelligent" actually means in practice. Is it using some form of ML for anomaly detection, or more heuristic-based decision support? With local-first architecture, training models on-device is a real constraint you'll need to think about carefully.

---
